reason: The item cleanly tests metric-dependent interpretation of ablation results (peak temperature vs time-to-peak) while also checking a separate but still propagation-relevant boundary condition (gap-dependent convection effect on triggering order). Decoys are largely single-fault and engineering-plausible, though the multi-correct format plus some wording (“absence of airflow,” “governs”) can introduce mild ambiguity and reduce purely linguistic neutrality. Overall it has strong discriminative power for experts who must resist collapsing multiple metrics into a single notion of “most important mechanism.”
